response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and web engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but is disqualified on multiple hard requirements for this role. Most critically, there is no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a listed mandatory requirement. Additionally, the candidate has no embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security domain experience, and C/C++ appears only marginally (a brief NI stint and a thesis project), far short of the strong UNIX C proficiency required. There is no evidence of Linux kernel work, MACsec, 802.1X, or any relevant networking stack knowledge.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, and some embedded contexts
Demonstrated low-latency and systems-adjacent work (DLang async I/O, high-performance services at Sociomantic)
Some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in academic projects
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement for the role
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, etc.)
Primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js/web; C and Linux kernel work is minimal and non-recent
